turn.plan.description: Judge a whole proposed turn under SRD 5.2.1 ACTION ECONOMY: action/bonus-action/reaction/free-interaction budgets, movement (crawling, standing from Prone, Grappled speed 0), Incapacitated, and the one-spell-slot-per-turn rule. Returns legal/illegal/cannot-adjudicate with citations; illegal verdicts name the first offending step. Scope: this checks the turn's economy only — it does NOT verify that a feature grants a given action (e.g. two-weapon fighting or Extra Attack prerequisites). Unmodeled conditions and out-of-range inputs (negative speed, spell level >9) return exit 2 rather than a guess.

conditions.description: active conditions by name (all 15 SRD conditions modeled; unknown names refused)

exhaustion_level.description: required to adjudicate movement when Exhaustion is present (reduces Speed 5 ft per level)

turn.options.description: Enumerate what remains legal this turn given the same state shape as turn.plan (no plan). Options carry feet_remaining, stand-up cost, crawl mode, and spell-slot availability. Consistency-tested against turn.plan: everything enumerated is accepted, everything past a boundary rejected.

reaction.available.description: Is taking a Reaction legal right now? Reactions refresh at the start of your own turn (not the round) and are usable on your own turn.

spent_since_turn_start.description: true if a Reaction was taken since the start of this creature's last turn

roll.compose.description: Compose net Advantage/Disadvantage from lists of sources: multiple sources never add dice, and any Advantage plus any Disadvantage yields a straight single-d20 roll regardless of counts. Optionally notes the reroll-one-die-only rule.

attack.modifiers.description: Compose an attack roll's Advantage/Disadvantage from both combatants' conditions and distance (Prone's 5-foot flip, Blinded, Restrained, Paralyzed, Stunned, Invisible with the seen exception, the Grappled attacker rule). Exhaustion is returned as a flat modifier, never Advantage. Genuinely ambiguous rules text (e.g. a seen Invisible target) returns cannot-adjudicate with the ambiguous sentence cited.

mage-hand.use.description: Adjudicate one proposed use of the Mage Hand cantrip: granted uses (manipulate, open unlocked, stow/retrieve, pour), prohibitions (attack, activate magic items), the 10-pound carry limit and 30-foot range leash. Uses outside the text's can/can't lists return cannot-adjudicate — the GM's call, honestly labeled.

kind.description: manipulate_object | open_unlocked | stow_retrieve_open | pour_vial | attack | activate_magic_item | anything else = GM territory

event.apply.description: The reducer (fold): apply one declared event to a state object and receive the verdict plus the stamped next_state (lineage: predecessor hash, causing event, rule ids, rule-vs-ruling kind). Event types: turn-start (budgets reset, Reaction returns), round-advance, action / bonus-action / reaction / free-interaction / move / stand-up (validated against budgets and conditions; may carry spell:{level} and concentration_on), condition-gained, condition-ended, damage (amount; crit:true for a Critical Hit; folds HP loss, Falling Unconscious, monster/massive-damage instant death, and death-save failures at 0 HP), heal (amount; restores HP and, from 0, consciousness + resets death saves), death-save (result: the rolled d20 1-20; folds success/failure counts, nat-1 double failure, nat-20 revival, stabilize, and death), ruling (GM discretion — recorded and tagged as discretion, never as a rule). The caller supplies every die result; srdcheck never produces an event, never advances time on its own, never generates randomness: the caller owns the loop; this is the fold.

state.description: current state per the adapter's state_schema.json (state travels; it never resides here)

crit.description: true if damage is from a Critical Hit (doubles death-save failures at 0 HP)

result.description: the rolled d20 (1-20) for a death-save event; the caller rolls, srdcheck folds

creature.valid.description: Is a name a valid SRD 5.2.1 creature? legal = yes (with CR + page citation); illegal = the name is SRD content but not a creature (e.g. a spell); cannot-adjudicate = not in the SRD at all (a 2014-only name, a typo, or third-party content — indistinguishable in this version).

creature.stats.description: Return a creature's Challenge Rating and XP with a page citation, for deterministic encounter-budget math. legal with data {name, cr, xp, citation} for a known creature; cannot-adjudicate otherwise.

encounter.xp-budget.description: XP budget per character for encounter building (SRD 5.2.1 p.202): given party level (1-20) and difficulty (low/moderate/high), returns the per-character XP budget with citation. Pass party_size to also get the total. Combine with creature.stats to check whether an encounter is within budget.

save.check.description: Adjudicate a saving throw against a DC (SRD 5.2.1 p.6). The CALLER rolls the d20 (RNG is out of scope, T6); srdcheck composes and resolves, cited. Optionally ability-typed and condition-aware: pass save_ability + saver_conditions to apply the codified overrides — auto-fail Str/Dex saves (Paralyzed/Petrified/Stunned/Unconscious), Disadvantage on Dex saves (Restrained), and Exhaustion's flat d20 penalty. With d20_result it returns {total, dc, success, roll[, auto_fail]}; without a d20 it composes and reports {roll, dc, net_modifier}. Refuses (exit 2) only if the DC is missing.

dc.description: the Difficulty Class to meet or exceed

d20_result.description: the rolled d20; omit to just get the composed roll mode + net modifier

modifier.description: total modifier added to the d20 (ability + proficiency + situational)

save_ability.description: str | dex | con | int | wis | cha — enables the ability-typed condition overrides (auto-fail Str/Dex, Restrained Dex disadvantage)

saver_conditions.description: the saving creature's active conditions (drives auto-fail / disadvantage)

exhaustion_level.description: applies Exhaustion's flat d20 penalty to the save

concentration.check.description: Compute the Concentration saving-throw DC when a concentrating creature takes damage: DC = max(10, damage // 2), capped at 30 (SRD 5.2.1 p.179). Optionally pass d20_result (+ con_modifier) to also resolve whether concentration holds. Returns data {dc[, total, success]}. The caller rolls; srdcheck folds.

d20_result.description: optional: the rolled d20 for the Constitution save

con_modifier.description: Constitution save modifier (used only if d20_result is given)
